stories.title: 'A roll of the dice' - Why is O'Sullivan using two cues at the Worlds?
bullets: Ronnie O'Sullivan is using two cues during the World Snooker Championship in 2026, a risky strategy he describes as a gamble.
O'Sullivan expressed nervousness about this decision, fearing it might make him look foolish in front of fans and competitors.
This unconventional approach could influence future players' strategies, highlighting the evolving nature of competitive snooker.

stories.title: World Snooker Championship scores and schedule
bullets: The 2026 World Snooker Championship is currently taking place, featuring Ronnie O'Sullivan and defending champion Zhao Xintong.
O'Sullivan aims for his eighth world title, while Xintong seeks to retain his championship status amid intense competition.
The tournament's outcome could redefine snooker history, particularly if O'Sullivan achieves his record-breaking title.

stories.title: Ken Griffin's deputy calls Zohran Mamdani's targeting of the billionaire 'shameful' in a companywide memo
bullets: Citadel's deputy criticized Zohran Mamdani's targeting of Ken Griffin in a companywide memo, as reported on April 23, 2026.
The memo defended Griffin's record in New York, labeling Mamdani's actions as 'shameful' and inappropriate.
This internal conflict highlights tensions within Citadel and the challenges of public scrutiny faced by high-profile executives.

stories.title: Recruiting is up. Now the US military wants to grow the force by another 44,500 troops.
bullets: The US military plans to increase its force by 44,500 troops due to improved recruiting and global demands as of April 2026.
This expansion aims to address rising threats and is part of a broader strategy to enhance military readiness and capabilities.
The recruitment boost reflects a shift in military priorities, emphasizing the need for a larger, more versatile force in a changing global landscape.

stories.title: An Unsettling Anti-Slavery Memorial
bullets: An unsettling anti-slavery memorial reveals the rapid erosion of national ideals and historical narratives.
The monument's story reflects societal struggles with acknowledging past injustices and their implications today.
This discussion encourages a reevaluation of how history is commemorated and the lessons it imparts for future generations.

stories.title: Israel Could Have What It Most Wants in Lebanon
bullets: Israel is presented with an opportunity to achieve its goals in Lebanon by cooperating with the Lebanese government.
The proposal involves disarming Hezbollah in exchange for territorial concessions, aiming for regional stability.
This potential collaboration could reshape Israeli-Lebanese relations and impact broader Middle Eastern dynamics.

stories.title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai says 75% of the company’s code is AI-generated
bullets: Google CEO Sundar Pichai announced that 75% of the company's new code is now AI-generated, a significant increase from 50% last fall.
This shift reflects a major productivity leap, as Google encourages all employees to embrace AI technologies in their workflows.
The reliance on AI for coding may redefine software development practices and influence the tech industry's future direction.

stories.title: The older you get, the harder it is to make friends at work. Here’s why
bullets: Research shows that making friends at work becomes increasingly difficult as individuals age, particularly for those over 40.
Olga Valadon, 54, highlights how work and family pressures limit opportunities for socializing among older employees.
Understanding these challenges can help organizations create better environments for fostering workplace relationships across all ages.

stories.title: An Interview with Surreal Salon 18 Winner, River Reishi
bullets: River Reishi, the winner of Surreal Salon 18, was interviewed about her artwork and experiences on April 9, 2026.
Selected by guest juror Swoon, Reishi discussed the significance of her work and the history of the Surreal Salon.
Her recognition highlights emerging talent in the art world, potentially influencing future exhibitions and artistic trends.

stories.title: William N. Copley "X-Rated (1972–1974)" @ Galerie Max Hetzler, Berlin
bullets: Galerie Max Hetzler in Berlin hosted the exhibition 'X-Rated (1972–1974)' featuring William N. Copley's works, which ended on April 22, 2026.
This was Copley's fourth exhibition at the gallery, showcasing his unique style and contributions to contemporary art.
The exhibition's conclusion reflects ongoing interest in Copley's work, potentially leading to future retrospectives.

stories.title: Trump pleads not guilty to 34 felony counts
bullets: Donald Trump pleaded not guilty to 34 felony counts related to business practices on April 25, 2026, in a New York court.
The charges include falsifying business records, which could lead to significant legal consequences and impact his political future.
This legal battle may influence public perception and voter sentiment as Trump prepares for potential future elections.

stories.title: Sonos’ big sale on refurbished speakers is about to end
bullets: Sonos is offering steep discounts on refurbished speakers until April 24, 2026, making high-quality audio more accessible.
The sale includes various models, with prices slashed by up to 30%, appealing to budget-conscious consumers looking for upgrades.
This promotion aims to boost sales ahead of new product launches and capitalize on the growing demand for home audio systems.

stories.title: Meta is laying off 10 percent of its staff
bullets: Meta plans to lay off approximately 8,000 employees, representing 10% of its workforce, starting in May 2026.
The layoffs follow significant investments in technology and restructuring efforts, as stated by Chief People Officer Janelle Gale.
This decision reflects ongoing challenges in the tech industry and may influence Meta's operational strategies moving forward.

stories.title: JPMorgan promoted 135 execs to managing director in banking and markets — see the list
bullets: JPMorgan promoted 135 executives to managing director in its banking and markets division, announced on April 23, 2026.
This annual promotion recognizes top performers, enhancing the firm's leadership and operational capabilities in a competitive market.
The promotions reflect JPMorgan's growth strategy and commitment to retaining talent in a challenging economic environment.

stories.title: The command overseeing US forces in the Middle East just got a third aircraft carrier — and a lot more firepower
bullets: The USS George H.W. Bush arrived in the US Central Command area on April 23, 2026, enhancing military presence in the Middle East.
This deployment adds significant combat power, with the carrier capable of launching airstrikes and supporting ground operations.
The increased military capability underscores the US commitment to regional stability amid ongoing tensions in the area.

stories.title: A former employee is suing MrBeast’s company for harassment, discrimination
bullets: A former employee filed a federal lawsuit against MrBeast's Beast Industries on April 21, 2026, alleging harassment and discrimination.
The lawsuit claims the company fostered a hostile work environment, particularly for women, and cites retaliation after maternity leave.
Beast Industries denies the allegations, stating they have evidence to refute the claims, which could impact their public image.

stories.title: Meta will lay off 10% of workforce, company told staff today
bullets: Meta announced on April 23, 2026, that it will lay off approximately 8,000 employees, about 10% of its workforce, on May 20.
The layoffs are part of a strategy to improve efficiency and will also include the cancellation of 6,000 open job positions.
This decision reflects ongoing challenges in the tech industry and aims to streamline operations amid rising operational costs.

stories.title: Brendan Carr’s war on wokeness targets inclusive children’s television
bullets: The FCC, led by Brendan Carr, is targeting children's television for content exploring gender identity, announced on April 23, 2026.
The Media Bureau is seeking public comments on the appropriateness of current TV ratings for such content, stirring controversy.
This initiative could reshape children's programming and reflects broader cultural debates over inclusivity and representation in media.

stories.title: We transformed our dated, dark kitchen into a bright, open-concept space — it turned out even better than I expected
bullets: A recent kitchen renovation transformed a dark space into a bright, open-concept area, completed by April 26, 2026.
The project included new cabinets, appliances, and improved natural light, enhancing functionality and aesthetics.
This DIY transformation reflects a growing trend in home improvement, emphasizing the value of modern, multifunctional living spaces.

stories.title: 18 celebrities who moved to Texas on why it's the best place for them
bullets: Celebrities like Elon Musk and Glen Powell have relocated to Texas, drawn by its lower cost of living compared to California.
Many stars cite Texas's favorable tax policies and lifestyle as reasons for their move, enhancing the state's cultural landscape.
This trend may influence Texas's economy and real estate market, attracting more high-profile residents and businesses.

stories.title: Thou Shalt Not Post the Ten Commandments in Classrooms
bullets: A federal court ruled that posting the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms violates the First Amendment on April 25, 2026.
The court emphasized that such endorsements of religious texts undermine the separation of church and state, impacting educational policies nationwide.
This ruling may influence similar cases across the U.S., potentially reshaping how schools approach religious content in classrooms.

stories.title: How Netanyahu Hurt America’s Jews
bullets: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is criticized for prioritizing his political agenda over the concerns of American Jews.
The article highlights a growing disconnect, stating that Netanyahu's actions have alienated many in the Jewish community.
This situation could impact U.S.-Israel relations and the support American Jews provide to Israel in the future.

stories.title: Why aircraft carriers are the best (and worst) place for laser weapons
bullets: The USS George HW Bush successfully shot down multiple drones using a 20 kilowatt laser weapon during tests in October 2025.
This live-fire test marks a significant milestone for the U.S. Navy, showcasing the potential of laser technology in naval defense systems.
The Navy aims to integrate laser weapons on more ships, enhancing defense capabilities against drone threats in future conflicts.

stories.title: Microsoft hit pause on carbon removal purchases. Now what?
bullets: Microsoft plans to pause its carbon removal purchases, affecting the carbon removal industry that it significantly supported in fiscal year 2025.
The company contracted 21 firms to remove 45 million tons of CO2, including projects in the Amazon and Midwest, driving market growth.
This pause raises concerns about the future of carbon removal initiatives, potentially stalling progress in climate change mitigation efforts.

stories.title: Tomora’s Come Closer is an ecstatic love letter to 90s dance music
bullets: Tomora's new album 'Come Closer' celebrates 90s dance music, gaining attention during Coachella 2026 for its unique sound and performance.
The band features two Nordic women performing on giant drums, captivating audiences with their energetic techno beats and visuals.
The album's release could revitalize interest in 90s dance music, appealing to both nostalgic fans and new listeners alike.

stories.title: Skylight’s 15-inch smart calendar is down to its lowest price to date
bullets: The Skylight Calendar 2, a 15-inch smart calendar, is now available for $259.99, marking a $40 discount until May 7th.
This device syncs multiple calendars, helping users manage their schedules more efficiently in a single interface.
The price drop may boost sales and increase consumer interest in smart home organization tools.

stories.title: Business leaders, including Elon Musk and Dana White, react to the shooting at the DC press dinner
bullets: Business leaders, including Elon Musk and Dana White, expressed their shock and condolences following the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25, 2026.
Musk emphasized the need for increased security measures, while White called for unity in the face of violence against public events.
Their reactions highlight the ongoing concerns about safety at high-profile gatherings, prompting discussions on security protocols for future events.

stories.title: Two Poems by Luis Muñoz
bullets: Luis Muñoz's two poems, translated by Garth Greenwell and Idra Novey, explore themes of love and existential questions in contemporary life.
The poems reflect on spontaneous love and societal inquiries, showcasing Muñoz's unique voice and emotional depth in modern poetry.
These translations contribute to the growing appreciation of Muñoz's work, potentially expanding his audience in the English-speaking literary community.

stories.title: Trump turns the WHCD shooting into a pitch for the White House ballroom
bullets: President Donald Trump used the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner to promote his ballroom project during a press conference on April 25, 2026.
He argued that enhancing security in the ballroom would prevent future threats, framing the project as a necessary safety measure.
This incident may influence public perception of Trump's initiatives, intertwining security concerns with his political agenda.
